Home Work Awards CV
Editor & Newsroom Leader

Holding power
accountable
since 2010.

Brad Racino is the editor-in-chief of Floodlight, a nonprofit newsroom investigating the powerful interests stalling climate action. Over 15 years in investigative journalism, he has worked in digital, TV, radio and print; produced work that has driven resignations, prompted congressional hearings and led to federal investigations; and earned more than 100 industry awards. Before leading newsrooms, he was a reporter, photographer and videographer who believed accountability journalism demands showing your work.

bradracino@gmail.com LinkedIn Bluesky X
AI & Innovation Brad builds AI tools for investigative newsrooms — from data scrapers to fact-checking workflows. See more →
Affiliations
IRE | SPJ | ACES | INN | NYCOG Board | Copilot Center of Excellence
Newsroom leadership
Floodlight
Editor-in-Chief
2026 — Present
Setting the editorial agenda for one of the country's leading nonprofit climate investigative newsrooms. Managing three reporters and their stories from conception to publication along with an ever-expanding national partnership and republication network.
NY Focus
Managing Editor
2024 — 2025
Doubled unique visitors within three months. Helped grow the newsroom from 8 to 15 staff and directly managed an editor and four reporters on long-running investigative series.
NY Cannabis Insider
Editor & Publisher
2021 — 2024
Built the publication from nothing — conceived the editorial vision, launched the site and grew a newsletter from zero to 5,000 subscribers in 12 months. Published 600+ stories and organized 12+ industry conferences while simultaneously reporting the biggest stories on the beat.
inewsource
Senior Reporter & Assistant Editor
2012 — 2021
Joined as an intern when the newsroom was four people and helped build it into a 17-person investigative operation. Wore every hat — reporter, photographer, videographer, web developer, assistant director, editor and partnership director. Built Transparify, a transparency tool that showed readers exactly how investigations were reported.
AI & Journalism

AI as investigative infrastructure.

Brad brings hands-on AI experience to investigative newsrooms — building tools, instituting policies and training reporters to use artificial intelligence as a key component of investigative reporting. He is constantly building bespoke tools to scrape, import and analyze data; synthesize disparate record formats; identify and strengthen reporter skill sets; and improve editing and fact-checking workflows. As part of Advance Media's Copilot Center of Excellence, he helped shape enterprise AI strategy for one of the largest media companies in the country.

Tools & platforms
Claude Claude API ChatGPT OpenAI API Gemini DALL·E Midjourney NotebookLM Qwen ElevenLabs
As an editor

As an editor at Floodlight, NY Focus, NY Cannabis Insider and inewsource, Brad has overseen investigations that drove resignations, changed policies and laws and improved quality of life for the afflicted.

Featured · 20-min mini-doc
Floodlight May 2026

Trump officials, billionaires and the quiet reshaping of America's public lands

My role
Editor overseeing the story and mini-documentary.
Shared by
Mother Jones · Inc.com · High Country News · Truthdig · RE:PUBLIC · Center for Political Awareness
Floodlight Apr 2026

Judges Overseeing Louisiana's Landmark Oil Cases Have Financial Stakes in Defendants

An investigation found that a dozen federal judges presiding over Louisiana's major environmental lawsuits against oil and gas companies held stocks, bonds, mineral rights or other financial ties to the defendants.

My role
Co-editor alongside an editor from Type Investigations. Oversaw reporter Garrett Hazelwood and orchestrated photo and video production with publication partner Verite News. Designed all illustrations used throughout the story.
Impact
Four newsroom partnership generating the first comprehensive accounting of financial conflicts among federal judges overseeing Louisiana's landmark climate and environmental litigation.
Shared by
The Nation · Type Investigations · WWNO/WRKO · Verite News · The Lens NOLA · Charlotte Observer · SEJ.org · The Mint Magazine · Louisiana Illuminator · Miami Herald · Rio Grande Guardian · Dundas Messenger
Floodlight Feb 2026

Thermal Drone Footage Shows Musk's AI Plant Flouting Clean Air Rules

First-of-its-kind drone imagery confirmed xAI was operating 15+ unpermitted gas turbines in Southaven, Miss. Ran on The Guardian's homepage. Millions of views.

My role
Editor-in-Chief. Oversaw reporter Evan Simon on Floodlight's most successful story. Managed partnership and legal review with The Guardian.
Impact
Went viral. A1 treatment on The Guardian homepage. Millions of Instagram views. Spawned 12-minute YouTube documentary. Photos to hang in a Paris art museum in 2027.
Shared by
The Guardian · Capital B · Grist · TruthDig · Latitude Media Tennessee Lookout · Mississippi Free Press · MLK50
Written about in
CNBC · Nieman Lab · Envirolink
NY Focus Jan 2026

FBI Wiretaps, Illegal Drugs and Dead Horses

State regulators sat on FBI evidence of a massive horse doping ring for years. We got the wiretaps.

My role
Series editor. Daily editing and rewriting with reporter Sam Mellins. Managed full fact-check cycle, legal review, photo direction.
Impact
Gaming Commission investigator resigned. Governor Hochul demanded accountability. State opened new investigation.
NY Cannabis Insider / Syracuse.com Mar 2024

‘Terrified to Criticize’: Accusations of Hostility and Retaliation Surface Against NYS Office of Cannabis Management

Leaked audio and interviews revealed a pattern of alleged selective enforcement and retaliation by the state’s cannabis regulator, prompting the governor to order a sweeping review.

My role
Editor. Oversaw reporter Wes Parnell on the investigation into the Office of Cannabis Management’s leadership and enforcement practices.
Impact
Chief Equity Officer resigned. Governor Hochul ordered sweeping review of the state cannabis program.
More editor work
  1. Jun 2025
    The Insurance Scheme That Could Leave New Yorkers With Mountains of Debt Insurer replaced. The state moved to replace Leading Edge Administrators as the insurer for home health workers statewide.
  2. Apr 2025
    For a Yonkers Political Dynasty, a Succession of Blurred Lines Three NYPA awards. First place, Local Government Story; second place, Investigative/In-Depth; second place, FOI.
  3. Dec 2024
    How Low Pay and Long Hours at the Assembly Hurt New York Staff received raises. Assembly salaries rose an average of 10%+ following the investigation.
  4. Nov 2024
    Investigating the Bronx Political Machine Forced new disclosures. Bronx Democrats filed over a dozen new or amended campaign finance disclosures.
As a reporter

From 2012 to 2024, Brad was a reporter, photographer and videographer at inewsource, NY Cannabis Insider and Syracuse.com — specializing in public records, data, visual storytelling and government accountability.

Featured · Documentary + multimedia
inewsource 2020

Rewired: An Experimental Brain Treatment Blows Up Two Lives

A Navy SEAL's psychotic break. A $10 million donation squandered. A year-long investigation into unproven electromagnetic therapy at UC San Diego.

My role
Wrote the main two-part investigation with Jill Castellano. Managed videographer Zoë Meyers on the documentary film. Designed the project website.
Impact
Two lawsuits filed. Internal UC San Diego investigation launched. Doctor found to have violated university policies. $10M gift mismanagement made public for the first time.
NY Cannabis Insider / Syracuse.com Sep 2023

NY's Testing Failures Expose Legal Weed Consumers to Unsafe Cannabis

A months-long investigation found top-selling weed strains sold at licensed New York dispensaries contained microbial contamination at 10–250 times the state's medical cannabis limits, exposing systemic failures from farmers to labs to state regulators.

My role
Lead reporter. Independent lab testing, records analysis and source development across New York's newly legal adult-use cannabis supply chain.
Impact
Went viral across the national cannabis industry. Won 1st Place for Distinguished State Government Coverage from the New York News Publishers Association for exposing "the state's shoddy testing that allowed contaminated marijuana to reach recreational consumers."
NY Cannabis Insider / Syracuse.com Dec 2022

Smoke and Mirrors: Inside the Murky $200M Effort to Kickstart NY's Marijuana Industry

How New York's dormitory authority became entangled in the state's cannabis licensing fund — and what happened to the money.

My role
Lead reporter. Public records investigation, financial document analysis and accountability reporting on a new and opaque state regulatory system.
Impact
CEO resigned shortly after publication. Reporting exposed financial mismanagement in New York's cannabis licensing apparatus.
inewsource Jun 2020

San Diego VA Moves Suicidal Veterans Off 'Life-Saving Treatment,' Against Doctor's Pleas

First in a long-running series on the San Diego VA and veteran suicides. The VA abruptly pulled suicidal veterans off ketamine treatments their doctors called life-saving — with fatal consequences.

My role
Lead reporter. Developed sources, obtained medical records and documented the VA's shifting public explanations across the multi-part series.
Impact
Federal investigation opened. The series prompted Rep. Scott Peters' office to demand answers; the VA's Office of the Inspector General launched a formal investigation shortly after. Reporting also documented the San Diego VA repeatedly contradicting itself about why veterans were taken off ketamine — discrepancies the agency refused to reconcile or explain.
inewsource Multimedia project

An Impossible Choice

A rare look inside a subacute care unit in Coronado, Calif. — one of hundreds statewide housing more than 4,000 life-support patients.

My role
Co-reported with Joanne Faryon. Also served as videographer, editor and web designer on the multimedia project.
Format
Documentary + multimedia project. Visual investigation into end-of-life care, combining photography, video and long-form reporting. Winner: IRE Medal, Meyer "Mike" Berger Award, National Edward R. Murrow Award (Audio & Video categories), National Emmy Award nomination.
More reporter work
  1. Jul 2019
    UCSD Doctor Resigns Amid Questions About China's Thousand Talents Program Subject resigned and left the country. Prompted renewed federal scrutiny of the Thousand Talents Program at UC San Diego.
  2. 2018–19
    Dangerous Human Research Alleged at San Diego VA Congressional hearing called. Main subject resigned from VA and UCSD; moved to Dubai.
  3. Dec 2018
    Security Gaps at San Diego Shipyards Put Billion-Dollar Navy Warships at Risk Congressman called for hearing on shipyard security protocols.

Memberships & Board Service

Media appearances & republication

Brad's work — as both reporter and editor — has appeared in or been cited by PBS NewsHour, The Washington Post, The Guardian, Der Spiegel, The Atlantic, The New Yorker, NBC News, MSNBC, Buzzfeed, Columbia Journalism Review, Center for Public Integrity, Marketplace, Tennessee Lookout, Mississippi Free Press, MLK50, Louisiana Illuminator, The Lens, WWNO, Capital B, Grist, Hell Gate, PBS MetroFocus, KQED, KPCC, KPBS, CBS8, NBC San Diego, Law360, San Diego Union-Tribune, The Capitol Pressroom, Lenfest Institute for Journalism, Syracuse.com, InvestigateTV, Florida Center for Investigative Reporting, RetractionWatch, News21, and others.

Curriculum Vitae
Download / Print
Hudson Valley, New York · bradracino@gmail.com · LinkedIn · Bluesky · X

Award-winning editor and investigative journalist with 15+ years of experience. Two IRE Medals, two Murrow Awards, a national Emmy nomination and 100+ career awards. Work has driven resignations, congressional hearings and federal investigations.

Skills

AI & Technology: Claude, ChatGPT, OpenAI API, Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, Google NotebookLM, DALL-E, Midjourney, Qwen, ElevenLabs, prompt and workflow design, editorial AI guardrails, AI-assisted editorial operations

Editorial & Leadership: Investigative editing, story development, newsroom management, team building and scaling, content strategy, editorial standards and ethics, legal review, fact-checking, partnership development, performance management

Production & Design: Adobe Premiere, Audition, Photoshop, Lightroom, Final Cut, WordPress, Airtable, photography, videography, audio production, multimedia storytelling

Audience & Analytics: Google Analytics (GA4), Parse.ly, Chartbeat, SEO optimization, A/B testing, newsletter development, audience growth strategy, subscription and subscriber retention

Technical: Basic Python (data scraping, database development), basic SQL, HTML/CSS, UX planning, API integration, content management systems

Experience
Editor-in-Chief · Floodlight
2026 – Present
  • Lead a nonprofit newsroom investigating the powerful interests stalling climate action
  • Refocused editorial strategy on high-stakes investigative projects, producing the organization's most-read investigation to date
  • Recruited key reporting talent and established a foundational AI policy framework
Managing Editor · New York Focus
2024 – 2025
  • Led creative and operational reset that doubled unique visitors within three months
  • Helped grow newsroom from 8 to 15 staff; managed and coached reporters and an editor on long-running investigative series
  • Brokered partnerships, increased newsletter frequency, iterated content formatting for maximum engagement
Editor & Publisher · NY Cannabis Insider (Advance Media / Syracuse.com)
2021 – 2024
  • Zero-to-one launch: conceived editorial vision, built the brand and grew it to statewide category leadership
  • Grew newsletter from zero to 5,000 subscribers in 12 months; stories generated millions of pageviews
  • Published 600+ stories; organized 12+ statewide conferences averaging 150–225 attendees
  • Collaborated directly with advertising, sales and marketing on sponsor packages and branded deliverables
  • Pioneered audience engagement tools including Subtext for direct reader communication and community building
  • Prototyped AI-assisted editorial ops (SEO, analysis, draft diagnostics, fact-checking); served on Advance Media's Copilot Center of Excellence for company-wide AI standards
Director, Editor & Senior Reporter · inewsource
2012 – 2021
  • Helped scale the organization from 4 to 17 staff; co-led hiring, training and five-year strategic vision
  • Managed reporters, visual journalists, social media and freelancers; trained team in web, radio and TV production
  • Established content syndication partnerships with NPR/PBS affiliates and CBS; frequent on-air contributor
  • Created innovative newsroom tools including public database platforms and the nationally recognized Transparify transparency tool
  • Instituted rigorous fact-checking, editorial standards and transparency practices; earned 90+ awards during tenure
